topic: cafe customer horror story — oat milk Karen

turns.content: i need to vent about a customer i had today before i literally lose my mind
okay so this woman comes in right. orders an oat milk flat white. fine. normal. i make it, beautiful latte art, genuinely one of my best pours today. hand it over.
Sounds like a solid start. I'm guessing it went downhill from there?

she takes ONE sip and goes 'this doesn't taste like oat milk' and i'm like... it is oat milk? i literally just poured it? and she goes 'no, oat milk is supposed to be sweeter, this tastes like regular milk, did you use regular milk?' ma'am. MA'AM.
Oh no. The classic "I know your job better than you" customer. Did she actually think you swapped the milk?

YES. she genuinely accused me of using dairy. like i would just... secretly give someone dairy??? do people not understand that could be an allergy thing?? i would NEVER. i showed her the carton. the actual physical carton of oat milk. she still wasn't convinced
You literally showed her the carton and she still didn't believe you? That's a whole new level. What did she want you to do at that point?

she wanted me to remake it. which fine whatever the customer is always right or whatever capitalist propaganda. so i remake it. SAME oat milk. same process. she tastes it and goes 'see, that's better, that's oat milk.' it was the EXACT same thing i cannot
The placebo effect of watching the barista make it in front of you is real, apparently. At least she was happy the second time around?

oh it gets better. she then asks if we have sugar free vanilla syrup. we do. i add it. she tastes it, and THEN says 'actually can you do half sweet.' so i have to remake it AGAIN. three flat whites for one woman. i reckon she just wanted to watch me suffer
Three remakes for one drink is genuinely painful. That's a lot of oat milk down the drain too. Did she at least tip?
lol. lol. no. she did not tip. she also told me on the way out that the cafe across the street does it better. cool. go there then??? why are you here torturing me for $27 an hour???
The parting shot about the other cafe is so unnecessary. Some people just want to have the worst possible interaction and then leave. At least you got a good story out of it.

honestly the only thing keeping me sane during shifts like this is knowing that one day the planet will be too hot for anyone to want hot coffee and then i'll be free. climate change is my retirement plan lol
That's the darkest silver lining I've ever heard, but I respect the coping mechanism. For what it's worth, you handled that way more professionally than most people would have.
thanks lol. i lowkey wanted to pour the third flat white on her head but i need this job so. restraint. anyway i feel better now. back to assignments i guess... the fun never stops
Glad the vent helped! Good luck with the assignments. And hey, maybe tomorrow's customers will actually be normal.
